element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Long-term goal spanning entire game; player gradually learns land clearing and crop mechanics
Core environmental modification mechanic; learned through repetition and visual feedback
Daily resource management; water requirements prevent growth but don't kill crops, reducing initial pressure
Requires daily feeding; chickens can die if left outside (storm/predators), introducing consequence
More complex than chickens; requires feeding, talking, brushing, milking; can sicken and die from neglect
Social events that break routine; optional but encouraged participation
Core social mechanic; marriage pursuit introduced as primary social goal
Long-term relationship goal requiring sustained interaction and affection accumulation (max 999 points)
Social gathering location where NPCs congregate; accessible only after dark

typical_relief_triggers: Livestock producing goods (milk from healthy cows)
typical_tension_triggers: Crop growth stalled by lack of water
Chicken death from storm or predators
Time pressure of daily tasks vs. available daylight
Relationship affection plateau with marriage candidates
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Slow, cyclical day-night rhythm with weekly and seasonal variation; tension builds gradually through cumulative neglect rather than acute threats; relief comes through successful completion of daily routines and long-term goal progression

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Foraging locations and hidden relationships vary by exploration; marriage candidates discovered through village interaction

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very permissive; crops don't die from lack of water (only stall), introducing mechanics without harsh punishment; village layout and basic routines learned
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity as livestock care becomes demanding; cow health degradation introduces consequence-based pressure; multiple daily tasks compete for limited daylight hours
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained management challenge; balancing farm productivity, relationship building, and livestock care; marriage goal pursues affection metrics requiring consistent engagement
notable_spikes: Multiple livestock and crops simultaneously requiring attention (time pressure spike)
Marriage candidate interaction requirements increasing (relationship management threshold)

notable_level_patterns.description: Entire game structured around repeating day-night cycle with specific time windows for activities; daylight hours constrain task completion; evening bar access provides social content after dark
Crop types, livestock behavior, and available activities vary by season; creates macro-level rhythm beyond daily cycles
Neglect consequences scale from soft (crop growth pause) to severe (livestock death); players learn cause-and-effect through graduated pressure
Marriage system requires sustained affection accumulation over extended play; individual interactions contribute to macro-goal progression across dozens of in-game days
